label: work
title: Procedure report
content:
INDICATION:  Patient with cirrhosis needing paracentesis.

EXAMINATION:  Ultrasound-guided paracentesis.

TECHNIQUE:  After an explanation of the risks, alternatives, and benefits,
written informed consent was obtained from the patient.  A limited ultrasound
of all four quadrants revealed a large amount of ascites.  A spot in the right
lower quadrant was selected and prepped and draped in the normal sterile
fashion.  A preprocedure timeout was performed to verify the correct patient
using two identifiers, and the correct procedure.  1% buffered lidocaine was
used to anesthetize the subcutaneous tissues.  A 5 ___ ___ catheter was
inserted into the peritoneal cavity and 7 L of clear, yellow ascites was
removed.  The patient tolerated the procedure well and there were no immediate
complications.

The attending radiologist, Dr. ___, was present and supervised the entire
procedure.

IMPRESSION:  Successful ultrasound-guided therapeutic paracentesis with
removal of 7 L of ascites.
